ain: noun. Alternative form of ayin (“Semitic letter”).
ait: noun. An island in a river, especially the River Thames in England.
ane: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Xârâcùù.
ani: noun. Any bird of the genus Crotophaga in the cuckoo family.
ant: noun. Any of various insects in the family Formicidae in the order Hymenoptera, typically living in large colonies composed almost entirely
ante: noun. A price or cost, as in up the ante.
att: noun. A subdivision of currency, equal to one hundredth of a Lao kip.
eat: verb. To ingest; to be ingested.
eta: noun. The seventh letter of the Modern Greek alphabet, the eighth in Old Greek.
etna: noun. A kind of small, portable cooking apparatus for which heat is furnished by a spirit lamp.
inti: noun. The currency of Peru between 1985 and 1991, replacing the sol.
nae: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Naka'ela.
neat: adjective. Clean, tidy; free from dirt or impurities.
net: noun. A mesh of string, cord or rope.
nett: adjective. Alternative spelling of net (remaining after expenses or deductions).
nit: noun. The egg of a louse.
tae: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tariana.
taint: noun. A contamination, decay or putrefaction, especially in food.
tat: noun. Cheap and vulgar tastelessness; sleaze.
tate: noun. Of video games, a vertical mode.
tea: noun. The tea plant (Camellia sinensis); a variety of this plant.
teat: noun. The projection of a mammary gland from which, on female therian mammals, milk is secreted.
ten: noun. NATO, ICAO, ITU & IMO radiotelephony code for 10, used only with o'clock to indicate direction.
tent: noun. A pavilion or portable lodge consisting of skins, canvas, or some strong cloth, stretched and sustained by poles, used for sheltering
tet: noun. Alternative form of teth (“Semitic letter”).
tin: noun. A malleable, ductile, metallic element, resistant to corrosion, with atomic number 50 and symbol Sn.
tine: noun. A spike or point on an implement or tool, especially a prong of a fork or a tooth of a comb.
tinea: noun. A fungal infection of the skin, known generally as ringworm.
tit: noun. A person's breast or nipple.
titan: noun. Something or someone of very large stature, greatness, or godliness.
titi: noun. Any New World monkey of the subfamily Callicebinae, native to South America, distinguished by their long soft fur.
titian: noun. A bright auburn colour, tinted with gold.
anet: noun. The herb dill; also dillseed.
ean: verb. To bring forth young; to yean.
iii: numeral. Ⅲ, the Roman numeral three (3).
neti: noun. Nasal irrigation performed using a neti pot as part of Hatha yoga.
nitta: noun. A tropical tree of the genus Parkia, having edible pods and seeds, especially Parkia filicoidea or Parkia biglobosa.
tai: noun. A fish, the Japanese bream.
tinta: noun. A kind of wine of a deep red color, chiefly from Galicia or Malaga in Spain.
tnt: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tontemboan.
